factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is stable as cash flows from operations have slightly decreased but remain positive.

evidence.text: Our cash flow used for operations of $(224.7) million during the three-month period ended December 28, 2018 was comparatively lower than the $46.9 million in cash flow from operations for the corresponding prior year period, due mainly to the increases in working capital (mainly accounts receivable) from the previous period offset by higher net earnings compared to the prior period.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive

factors.summary: The economic environment is a headwind due to the uncertainty related to U.S. government shutdowns and potential impacts on discretionary income and financial markets.

evidence.text: Additional and/or extended U.S. government shutdowns or any related under-staffing of the government departments or agencies that interact with our business could result in program cancellations, disruptions and/or stop work orders, could limit the government’s ability to effectively progress programs and make timely payments, and could limit our ability to perform on our existing U.S. government contracts and successfully compete for new work.
evidence.subsection: Additional and/or extended government shutdowns may negatively impact our business.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic uncertainty due to government shutdowns poses a headwind.

factors.summary: The company is showing signs of deleveraging, as indicated by the reduction in cash used for operations and the decrease in letters of credit outstanding. However, debt leverage remains stable with manageable interest expense, suggesting a balanced approach to debt management.

evidence.text: The amount of cash and cash equivalents at December 28, 2018 represented an increase of $114.5 million from $772.2 million at September 28, 2018. Additionally, cash and cash equivalents were $27.2 million relating to discontinued operations at the end of the period, a decrease of $3.4 million from the prior period.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES
